---
title: "Quantifying comprehensive marine transportation emissions using data fusion"
author: "emLab, UCSB and GFW"
date: last-modified
date-format: "[Last Updated on] MMMM DD, YYYY"
format:
  pdf:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    colorlinks: true
    fig-pos: H
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
editor: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, results = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE, cache = FALSE}
#| label: document-setup

# Set default chunk options
knitr::opts_chunk$set(message = FALSE, warning = FALSE, echo = FALSE, dev = "png", fig.path = "figures/")

# Load librariers
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(tidyr)
library(targets)

# Set consistent theme for all plots
theme_plot <- function(){
  theme_minimal() %+replace%
    theme(axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold"),
          axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 90,
                                      face = "bold",
                                      vjust = 3),
          strip.text.x = element_text(angle = 0,
                                      face = "bold"),
          strip.text.y = element_text(angle = 0,
                                      face = "bold"))}

# Set map projection for all map figures
map_projection <- "+proj=eqearth"

# Set consistent theme for maps
# Based loosely off fishwatchr::theme_gfw_map()
# https://github.com/GlobalFishingWatch/fishwatchr/blob/master/R/theme_gfw_map.R
theme_map <- function(){
  theme_minimal() %+replace%
    theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "black"),
          legend.position = "bottom",
          legend.direction = "horizontal",
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
          panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "black"),
          panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "black"),
          axis.text.x = element_blank(),
          axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
          axis.title.y = element_blank(),
          axis.text.y = element_blank(),
          axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
          strip.background = element_rect(fill=NA,color=NA),
          plot.margin = unit(c(0.25,0,0,0), "cm"))
}

# Create bounding box of globe, for making black map backgrounds
lats <- c(90:-90, -90:90, 90)
longs <- c(rep(c(180, -180), each = 181), 180)
world_bbox_sf <- 
  list(cbind(longs, lats)) %>%
  sf::st_polygon() %>%
  sf::st_sfc( # create sf geometry list column
    crs = "+proj=longlat +ellps=WGS84 +datum=WGS84 +no_defs"
  ) %>% 
  sf::st_sf() %>%
  sf::st_transform(map_projection)

land <- rnaturalearth::ne_countries(returnclass = "sf") |>
  dplyr::select(geometry)

# Convert lat/lon pixel area to square km
# https://stackoverflow.com/a/1253545
# Need function to convert degrees to radians
# https://stackoverflow.com/q/32370485
deg2rad <- function(deg) {(deg * pi) / (180)}

# Given a latitude, what is a pixel's area?
# https://stackoverflow.com/a/1253545
lat_to_area <- function(lat){
  110.574*(111.320*cos(deg2rad(lat)))
}

# 
data_directory_base <-  ifelse(Sys.info()["nodename"] == "quebracho" | Sys.info()["nodename"] == "sequoia",
                               "/home/emlab",
                               # Otherwise, set the directory for local machines based on the OS
                               # If using Mac OS, the directory will be automatically set as follows
                               ifelse(Sys.info()["sysname"]=="Darwin",
                                      "/Users/Shared/nextcloud/emLab",
                                      # If using Windows, the directory will be automatically set as follows
                                      ifelse(Sys.info()["sysname"]=="Windows",
                                             "G:/Shared\ drives/nextcloud/emLab",
                                             # If using Linux, will need to manually modify the following directory path based on their user name
                                             # Replace your_username with your local machine user name
                                             "/home/your_username/Nextcloud")))

project_directory <- glue::glue("{data_directory_base}/projects/current-projects/paper-ocean-ghg")

# Read and assign each object explicitly
s1_knn_ratios_within_footprint <- targets::tar_read("s1_knn_ratios_within_footprint",
                                                    store = targets::tar_config_get("store", 
                                                                                    project = "base_pipeline"))

s1_knn_ratios_outside_footprint <- targets::tar_read("s1_knn_ratios_outside_footprint",
                                                     store = targets::tar_config_get("store", 
                                                                                     project = "base_pipeline"))

s1_summarized_dark_fleet_ratios_spatial <- targets::tar_read("s1_summarized_dark_fleet_ratios_spatial",
                                                             store = targets::tar_config_get("store", 
                                                                                             project = "base_pipeline"))

s1_summarized_dark_fleet_model_results_emissions_year <- targets::tar_read("s1_summarized_dark_fleet_model_results_emissions_year",
                                                                           store = targets::tar_config_get("store", 
                                                                                                           project = "base_pipeline"))



# Load target objects
# Set targets store to appropriate GRIT/Nextcloud directory
# tar_config_set(project = "base_pipeline",
#                script= "_targets.R",
#                store = glue::glue("{project_directory}/data/_targets"))
# tar_load(c(monthly_ais_vessels_and_ratios_by_pixel,
#            monthly_ais_vessels_and_ratios))
```

```{r cache = FALSE}
#| label: load-data

# Annual CO2 emissions for the transport sector, from IEA
# "Global CO2 emissions from transport by sub-sector in the Net Zero Scenario, 2000-2030"
# Citation: IEA (2022), Global CO2 emissions from transport by sub-sector in the Net Zero Scenario, 2000-2030, IEA, Paris https://www.iea.org/data-and-statistics/charts/global-co2-emissions-from-transport-by-sub-sector-in-the-net-zero-scenario-2000-2030, Licence: CC BY 4.0
# https://www.iea.org/data-and-statistics/charts/global-co2-emissions-from-transport-by-sub-sector-in-the-net-zero-scenario-2000-2030-2
# Note that the CSV from EIA was very messy; needed to manually clean it up quite a bit
annual_iea_tranportation_emissions <- glue::glue("{project_directory}/data/raw/iea/global-co2-emissions-from-transport-by-sub-sector-in-the-net-zero-scenario-2000-2030.csv") |>
  readr::read_csv() |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-year,
                      names_to = "sector",
                      values_to = "emissions_co2_gt") |>
  # Only need years that correspond to our dataset
  dplyr::filter(year>=2016,
                year<=2024)|> 
  # Convert gigatons to metric tonnes
  dplyr::mutate(emissions_co2_mt = emissions_co2_gt*1e9) |>
  dplyr::select(-emissions_co2_gt)

# EDGAR - Emissions Database for Global Atmospheric Research
# From the European Commission
# Annual totals by sector and country (1970-2023)
# Downloaded from here: https://edgar.jrc.ec.europa.eu/dataset_ghg2024#p1
# For each substance emission time series (1970-2023) by sector and country are provided in an overview table (.xlsx). Emission country totals are expressed in kton substance / year. The IPCC 1996 and 2006 codes are used for specification of the sectors.
annual_edgar_tranportation_emissions <- glue::glue("{project_directory}/data/raw/IEA_EDGAR_CO2_1970_2023/IEA_EDGAR_CO2_1970_2023.xlsx") |>
  readxl::read_excel(sheet = "IPCC 2006", skip = 9) |>
  dplyr::select(-c(IPCC_annex,C_group_IM24_sh,Country_code_A3,Name,ipcc_code_2006_for_standard_report,Substance,fossil_bio)) |>
  dplyr::rename(sector = ipcc_code_2006_for_standard_report_name) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = -c(sector),
                      names_to = "year",
                      values_to = "emissions_co2_kt") |>
  dplyr::mutate(year = stringr::str_remove_all(year,"Y_") |>
                  as.numeric(),
                emissions_co2_mt = emissions_co2_kt * 1e3) |>
  dplyr::select(-emissions_co2_kt) |>
  dplyr::mutate(land_based_tranpsportation = ifelse(sector %in% c("Civil Aviation",
                                                                  "Road Transportation no resuspension",
                                                                  "Other Transportation",
                                                                  "Railways"), TRUE,FALSE)) |>
  # Only need years that correspond to our dataset
  dplyr::filter(year>=2016,
                year<=2024)


annual_emissions_all_pollutants <- glue::glue("{project_directory}/data/processed/annual_emissions_all_pollutants.csv") |>
  readr::read_csv() |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-c(year,fishing),
                      names_to = "pollutant",
                      values_to = "emissions_mt") |>
  dplyr::mutate(fleet = ifelse(stringr::str_detect(pollutant,"dark"),"Non-broadcasting","AIS-broadcasting")) |>
  dplyr::mutate(pollutant = stringr::str_remove_all(pollutant,"emissions_") |>
                  stringr::str_remove_all("_mt") |>
                  stringr::str_remove_all("_dark")) |>
  # Don't use 2025 data, since the year is incomplete
  dplyr::filter(year < 2025)

annual_emissions_all_pollutants_with_total <- annual_emissions_all_pollutants |>
  # Add total, that aggregates across broadcasting and non-broadcasting
  bind_rows(annual_emissions_all_pollutants |> 
              dplyr::group_by(year,fishing,pollutant) |> 
              dplyr::summarize(emissions_mt = sum(emissions_mt)) |> 
              dplyr::ungroup() |> 
              dplyr::mutate(fleet = "Total"))

# Aggregate emissions across fishing/non-fishing
annual_emissions <- annual_emissions_all_pollutants_with_total |>
  dplyr::group_by(year, fleet, pollutant) |>
  dplyr::summarise(emissions_mt = sum(emissions_mt)) |>
  dplyr::ungroup()

# Annual AIS-based CO2 emissions, hours, average speed, and main engine power by vessel
annual_ais_co2_emissions_by_vessel <- glue::glue("{project_directory}/data/processed/annual_ais_co2_emissions_by_vessel.csv") |>
  readr::read_csv() |>
  # Don't use 2025 data, since the year is incomplete
  dplyr::filter(year < 2025)

# Annual spatial CO2 emissions, for AIS and dark fleets
annual_spatial_co2_emissions_ais_dark <-  glue::glue("{project_directory}/data/processed/annual_spatial_co2_emissions_ais_dark.csv") |>
  readr::read_csv() |>
  # Don't use 2025 data, since the year is incomplete
  dplyr::filter(year < 2025)

# Annual global emissions and number of unique vessels by receiver type (satellite; dynamic; or terrestrial)
annual_global_emissions_by_receiver_type <-  glue::glue("{project_directory}/data/processed/annual_global_emissions_by_receiver_type.csv") |>
  readr::read_csv() |>
  # Don't include unknown receiver type
  dplyr::filter(receiver_type != "unknown") |>
  # Fill in missing values with 0s
  tidyr::complete(
    receiver_type,
    tidyr::nesting(year),
    fill = list(emissions_co2_mt = 0, n_distinct_ssvid = 0)
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(receiver_type = stringr::str_to_sentence(receiver_type))  |>
  # Don't use 2025 data, since the year is incomplete
  dplyr::filter(year < 2025)

# Annual global emissions and number of unique vessels by receiver type (satellite; dynamic; or terrestrial) and flag
annual_global_emissions_by_receiver_type_and_flag <-  glue::glue("{project_directory}/data/processed/annual_global_emissions_by_receiver_type_and_flag.csv") |>
  readr::read_csv() |>
  # Add column name for country name based on flag
  
  dplyr::mutate(flag = countrycode::countrycode(flag,
                                                origin = "iso3c",
                                                destination = "country.name")) |>
  # Don't include unknown receiver type
  dplyr::filter(receiver_type != "unknown") |>
  dplyr::mutate(receiver_type = stringr::str_to_sentence(receiver_type) |>
                  forcats::fct_relevel("Satellite",
                                       "Terrestrial",
                                       "Dynamic")) |>
  # Fill in missing values with 0s
  tidyr::complete(
    receiver_type,
    tidyr::nesting(year,flag),
    fill = list(emissions_co2_mt = 0, n_distinct_ssvid = 0)
  )  |>
  # Don't use 2025 data, since the year is incomplete
  dplyr::filter(year < 2025)

# Annual spatial CO2 emissions vt receiver type  (satellite; dynamic; or terrestrial)
annual_spatial_emissions_by_receiver_type <-  glue::glue("{project_directory}/data/processed/annual_spatial_emissions_by_receiver_type.csv") |>
  readr::read_csv() |>
  dplyr::mutate(receiver_type = stringr::str_to_sentence(receiver_type) |>
                  forcats::fct_relevel("Satellite",
                                       "Terrestrial",
                                       "Dynamic")) |>
  # Don't use 2025 data, since the year is incomplete
  dplyr::filter(year < 2025)

# Annual extrapolation of AIS activity to dark activity
annual_ais_to_dark_activity_extrapolation <-  glue::glue("{project_directory}/data/processed/annual_ais_to_dark_activity_extrapolation.csv") |>
  readr::read_csv() |>
  dplyr::select(-c(average_speed_knots_dark,average_speed_knots)) |>
  dplyr::mutate(across(-c(year,fishing,length_size_class_percentile),
                       ~tidyr::replace_na(.,0))) |>
  # Don't use 2025 data, since the year is incomplete
  dplyr::filter(year < 2025)

# Total spatial emissions by pollutant for 2024
total_spatial_emissions_by_pollutant_2024 <- glue::glue("{project_directory}/data/processed/total_spatial_emissions_by_pollutant_2024.csv") |>
  readr::read_csv() |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-c(lat_bin,lon_bin),
                      names_to = "pollutant",
                      values_to = "emissions_mt") |>
  dplyr::mutate(pollutant = stringr::str_remove_all(pollutant,
                                                    "emissions_") |>
                  stringr::str_remove_all("_total_mt") |>
                  stringr::str_to_upper() |>
                  stringr::str_replace_all("_","."),
                pollutant = ifelse(pollutant == "VOCS","VOCs",pollutant))

# Total monthly non-spatial emissions by pollutant
total_monthly_emissions_by_pollutant <- glue::glue("{project_directory}/data/processed/total_monthly_emissions_by_pollutant.csv") |>
  readr::read_csv() |>
  dplyr::mutate(month = lubridate::ymd(month)) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-month,
                      names_to = "pollutant",
                      values_to = "emissions_mt") |>
  dplyr::mutate(pollutant = stringr::str_remove_all(pollutant,
                                                    "emissions_") |>
                  stringr::str_remove_all("_total_mt") |>
                  stringr::str_to_upper() |>
                  stringr::str_replace_all("_","."),
                pollutant = ifelse(pollutant == "VOCS","VOCs",pollutant)) |>
  # Don't use 2025 data, since the year is incomplete
  dplyr::filter(lubridate::year(month) < 2025)


# Downloaded from UNTAD Trade-and-Transport Dataset
# https://unctadstat.unctad.org/datacentre/dataviewer/US.TransportCosts
# Filtered to just at-sea transport, where destination and source are both "World",
# And looking across all commodities
unctad_annual_global_trade_value_data_maritime_transport <- glue::glue("{project_directory}/data/raw/unctad/US.TransportCosts_20250311_204332.csv") |>
  readr::read_csv() |>
  dplyr::filter(Destination_Label == "World") |>
  dplyr::select(-Destination_Label) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(dplyr::everything(),
                      values_to = "value_usd") |>
  dplyr::mutate(year = stringr::str_remove_all(name,
                                               "_FOB_value_US_Value") |>
                  as.numeric()) |>
  dplyr::select(-name)
```

# Paper figures

### Figure 1 panel A

```{r}
# Calculate annual terrestrial transportation emissions from EDGAR
land_based_transportation_emissions_edgar <- annual_edgar_tranportation_emissions |> 
  dplyr::filter(land_based_tranpsportation) |>
  dplyr::group_by(year) |>
  dplyr::summarise(emissions_co2_mt = sum(emissions_co2_mt,na.rm=TRUE)) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::mutate(data_source = "Terrestrial (EDGAR)")

# Calculate annual terrestrial transportation emissions from IEA
land_based_transportation_emissions_iea <- annual_iea_tranportation_emissions |> 
  dplyr::filter(sector!="Shipping") |>
  dplyr::group_by(year) |>
  dplyr::summarise(emissions_co2_mt = sum(emissions_co2_mt,na.rm=TRUE)) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::mutate(data_source = "Terrestrial (IEA)")

all_tranportation_data <- land_based_transportation_emissions_edgar |>
  dplyr::bind_rows(land_based_transportation_emissions_iea) |>
  dplyr::bind_rows(annual_emissions |> 
                     dplyr::filter(pollutant == "co2",
                                   fleet == "Total") |>
                     dplyr::rename(emissions_co2_mt = emissions_mt) |>
                     dplyr::mutate(data_source = "Marine (AIS plus S1)") |>
                     dplyr::select(-c(fleet,pollutant)))


# Get 2016 values for time-series normalization
all_tranportation_data_starting <- all_tranportation_data |>
  dplyr::filter(year == 2016) |>
  dplyr::mutate(emissions_co2_mt_starting = emissions_co2_mt) |>
  dplyr::select(-c(year,emissions_co2_mt))

# Use Okabe-Ito colorblind-friendly colors
all_data_source_color_palette = c("Marine (AIS plus S1)" = paletteer::paletteer_d("colorblindr::OkabeIto")[[5]],
                                  "AIS plus S1" = paletteer::paletteer_d("colorblindr::OkabeIto")[[5]],
                                  "Terrestrial (EDGAR)" = paletteer::paletteer_d("colorblindr::OkabeIto")[[6]],
                                  "Terrestrial (IEA)" = paletteer::paletteer_d("colorblindr::OkabeIto")[[1]],
                                  "AIS (broadcasting)" = paletteer::paletteer_d("colorblindr::OkabeIto")[[3]],
                                  "S1 (non-broadcasting)" = paletteer::paletteer_d("colorblindr::OkabeIto")[[7]],
                                  "EDGAR" = paletteer::paletteer_d("colorblindr::OkabeIto")[[8]],
                                  "EIA" = paletteer::paletteer_d("colorblindr::OkabeIto")[[4]])

all_tranportation_data_normalized <- all_tranportation_data |>
  dplyr::inner_join(all_tranportation_data_starting, by = "data_source")|>
  dplyr::mutate(emissions_co2_mt_normalized = emissions_co2_mt/emissions_co2_mt_starting-1) 

# Summarize total emissions by data source for 2023, for putting on figure
emissions_comparison_figure <- all_tranportation_data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = year,y= emissions_co2_mt, color = data_source))  +
  geom_line() + 
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(all_tranportation_data_normalized$year),
                     limits = c(2016,2024)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::unit_format(unit = "B", scale = 1e-9), limits = c(0,NA)) +
  theme_plot() +
  labs(x = "",
       y = "",
       title = "Annual transportation CO2 emissions\n(billion MT)",
       color = "Data source") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position="right",
        legend.direction="vertical")+
  scale_color_manual(values = all_data_source_color_palette,
                     breaks = c("Terrestrial (EDGAR)",
                                "Terrestrial (IEA)",
                                "Marine (AIS plus S1)"))

emissions_comparison_figure_normalized <- all_tranportation_data_normalized |>
  ggplot(aes(x = year,y= emissions_co2_mt_normalized, color = data_source))  +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype=2) +
  geom_line() + 
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(all_tranportation_data_normalized$year),
                     limits = c(2016,2024)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent,
                     limits = c(-0.2,0.35)) +
  theme_plot() +
  labs(x = "",
       y = "",
       title = "Relative change in transportation CO2 emissions\nfrom 2016 baseline",
       color = "Data source") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position="right",
        legend.direction="vertical")+
  scale_color_manual(values = all_data_source_color_palette,
                     breaks = c("Marine (AIS plus S1)",
                                "Terrestrial (EDGAR)",
                                "Terrestrial (IEA)"))
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-summary-stat-comparison-2023-gfw-edgar
#| tbl-cap: Comparison of 2023 data between marine transportation emissions from GFW and terrestrial transportation emissions from EDGAR.

edgar_comparison <- all_tranportation_data_normalized |>
  dplyr::filter(data_source %in% c("Marine (AIS plus S1)",
                                   "Terrestrial (EDGAR)")) |>
  dplyr::filter(year == 2023) |>
  dplyr::select(-year) |>
  dplyr::mutate(data_source = ifelse(data_source == "Marine (AIS plus S1)","ocean_gfw","land_edgar"))|>
  dplyr::select(-c(emissions_co2_mt_starting)) |>
  dplyr::rename(emissions_normalized_from_2016 = emissions_co2_mt_normalized) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(values_from = c(emissions_co2_mt,
                                     emissions_normalized_from_2016),
                     names_from = data_source) |>
  dplyr::mutate(normalized_ocean_over_land = as.character(signif(emissions_normalized_from_2016_ocean_gfw/emissions_normalized_from_2016_land_edgar,2)),
                fraction_ocean_over_total = emissions_co2_mt_ocean_gfw / (emissions_co2_mt_ocean_gfw+emissions_co2_mt_land_edgar)) |>
  dplyr::mutate(across(c(emissions_normalized_from_2016_ocean_gfw,
                         emissions_normalized_from_2016_land_edgar,
                         fraction_ocean_over_total),
                       ~scales::percent(.))) |>
  dplyr::mutate(across(c(emissions_co2_mt_land_edgar,
                         emissions_co2_mt_ocean_gfw),
                       ~as.character(signif(./1e9,2)))) |>
  dplyr::rename(emissions_co2_billion_mt_land_edgar = emissions_co2_mt_land_edgar,
                emissions_co2_billion_mt_ocean_gfw = emissions_co2_mt_ocean_gfw) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = everything())

knitr::kable(edgar_comparison)
```


### Figure 1 panel B

```{r}
# Calculate the total percent change in emissions by fleet
total_percent_change_by_fleet <- annual_emissions |>
  dplyr::filter(year %in% c(2016,2024),
                fleet != "Total")  |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = year,
                     values_from = emissions_mt) |>
  dplyr::arrange(pollutant) |>
  dplyr::group_by(pollutant) |>
  dplyr::mutate(percent_total_2024 = `2024`/sum(`2024`))  |>
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  split( .[,c("pollutant")]) %>%
  purrr::map_df(janitor::adorn_totals,fill = NA) |>
  tidyr::fill(pollutant,.direction = "down") |>
  # janitor::adorn_totals("row") |>
  dplyr::mutate(percent_total_2024 = scales::percent(percent_total_2024)) |>
  dplyr::mutate(percent_change = 100*(`2024` - `2016`)/`2016`) |>
  dplyr::mutate(fleet = dplyr::case_when(fleet == "Total" ~ "AIS plus S1",
                                         fleet == "AIS-broadcasting" ~ "AIS (broadcasting)",
                                         TRUE ~ "S1 (non-broadcasting)"))

total_annual_emissions_by_fleet_fig <- annual_emissions  |>
  dplyr::filter(pollutant == "co2")|>
  dplyr::mutate(fleet = dplyr::case_when(fleet == "Total" ~ "AIS plus S1",
                                         fleet == "AIS-broadcasting" ~ "AIS (broadcasting)",
                                         TRUE ~ "S1 (non-broadcasting)")) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = emissions_mt, color = fleet)) +
  geom_line() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::unit_format(unit = "B", scale = 1e-9),
                     limits = c(0,1.5e9)) + 
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(annual_emissions$year),
                     limits = c(2016,2024)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = all_data_source_color_palette,
                     breaks = c("AIS plus S1","AIS (broadcasting)","S1 (non-broadcasting)"))+
  theme_plot() +
  labs(x = "",
       y = "",
       title = "Annual marine CO2 emissions\n(billion MT)",
       color = "Data source") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "right")
```

## Figure 1 panel C

```{r}
# Get 2016 values for time-series normalization
annual_co2_emissions_starting <- annual_emissions |>
  dplyr::filter(pollutant == "co2",
                year == 2016) |>
  dplyr::mutate(emissions_co2_mt_starting = emissions_mt) |>
  dplyr::select(-c(year,emissions_mt))

normalized_annual_emissions_by_fleet_fig <- annual_emissions |>
  dplyr::filter(pollutant == "co2") |>
  dplyr::left_join(annual_co2_emissions_starting, by = "fleet") |>
  dplyr::mutate(emissions_co2_mt_normalized = emissions_mt/emissions_co2_mt_starting-1) |>
  dplyr::mutate(fleet = dplyr::case_when(fleet == "Total" ~ "AIS plus S1",
                                         fleet == "AIS-broadcasting" ~ "AIS (broadcasting)",
                                         TRUE ~ "S1 (non-broadcasting)")) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = emissions_co2_mt_normalized, color = fleet)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = 2) +
  geom_line() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent,
                     limits = c(-0.2,0.35)) + 
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(annual_emissions$year),
                     limits = c(2016,2024)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = all_data_source_color_palette,
                     breaks = c("AIS (broadcasting)","AIS plus S1","S1 (non-broadcasting)"))+
  theme_plot() +
  labs(x = "",
       y = "",
       title = "Relative change in marine CO2 emissions\nfrom 2016 baseline",
       color = "Data source") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "right")
```


### Figure 1 combined

```{r}
#| label: fig-total-annual-emissions-by-data-source
#| fig-cap: (A) Annual transportation sector CO2 emissions, with each line colored based on the data source and whether it is marine or terrestrial transportation. (B) Relative change in annual transportation sector CO2 emissions, with each line colored based on the data source and whether it is marine or terrestrial transportation. (C) Annual marine CO2 emissions, with each line colored based on the data source. (D) Relative change in annual marine CO2 emissions from a 2016 baseline, with each line colored based on the data source.
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 7.5
cowplot::plot_grid(
  emissions_comparison_figure,
  total_annual_emissions_by_fleet_fig,
  emissions_comparison_figure_normalized,
  normalized_annual_emissions_by_fleet_fig,
  ncol=2,
  align="v",
  axis = "lr",
  labels=c("A","C","B","D")
)
```

# Supplementary figure - Comparing sector-specific emissions by data source (absolute)

```{r}
transportation_emissions_edgar_full_sectors <- annual_edgar_tranportation_emissions |> 
  dplyr::filter(land_based_tranpsportation | sector=="Water-borne Navigation") |>
  dplyr::group_by(year,sector) |>
  dplyr::summarise(emissions_co2_mt = sum(emissions_co2_mt,na.rm=TRUE)) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::mutate(data_source = "EDGAR")

transportation_emissions_iea_full_sectors <- annual_iea_tranportation_emissions |> 
  dplyr::group_by(year,sector) |>
  dplyr::summarise(emissions_co2_mt = sum(emissions_co2_mt,na.rm=TRUE)) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::mutate(data_source = "EIA")

all_tranportation_data_full_sectors <- transportation_emissions_edgar_full_sectors |>
  dplyr::bind_rows(transportation_emissions_iea_full_sectors) |>
  dplyr::bind_rows(annual_emissions |> 
                     dplyr::filter(pollutant == "co2",
                                   fleet == "Total") |>
                     dplyr::rename(emissions_co2_mt = emissions_mt) |>
                     dplyr::mutate(data_source = "AIS plus S1") |>
                     dplyr::select(-fleet)) |>
  dplyr::mutate(sector = case_when(sector %in% c("Civil Aviation","Aviation") ~ "Aviation",
                                   sector %in% c("Railways","Rail") ~ "Railways",
                                   sector %in% c("Road Transportation no resuspension","Road") ~ "Road",
                                   sector %in% c("Other Transportation","Pipeline transport") ~ "Other",
                                   TRUE ~ "Marine"))

all_tranportation_data_full_sectors |>
  ggplot(aes(x = year,y= emissions_co2_mt, color = data_source))  +
  geom_line() + 
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(all_tranportation_data_full_sectors$year),
                     limits = c(2016,2024)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::unit_format(unit = "B", scale = 1e-9), limits = c(0,NA)) +
  theme_plot() +
  labs(x = "",
       y = "Annual emissions (billion MT)",
       title = "",
       color = "Data source") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position="right",
        legend.direction="vertical") +
  facet_wrap(sector~.,ncol=2,scales="free") +
  scale_color_manual(values = all_data_source_color_palette,
                     breaks = c("AIS plus S1",
                                "EIA",
                                "EDGAR"))
```

# Supplementary figure - Comparing sector-specific emissions by data source (normalized)

```{r}
all_tranportation_data_full_sectors_starting <- all_tranportation_data_full_sectors |>
  dplyr::filter(year == 2016) |>
  dplyr::mutate(emissions_co2_mt_starting = emissions_co2_mt) |>
  dplyr::select(-c(year,emissions_co2_mt))

all_tranportation_data_full_sectors |>
  left_join(all_tranportation_data_full_sectors_starting, by = c("sector","data_source")) |>
  dplyr::mutate(emissions_co2_mt_normalized = emissions_co2_mt/emissions_co2_mt_starting-1) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = year,y= emissions_co2_mt_normalized, color = data_source))  +
  geom_hline(yintercept=0,linetype=2) +
  geom_line() + 
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(all_tranportation_data_full_sectors$year),
                     limits = c(2016,2024)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  theme_plot() +
  labs(x = "",
       y = "Annual emissions (billion MT)",
       title = "",
       color = "Data source") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position="right",
        legend.direction="vertical") +
  facet_wrap(sector~.,ncol=2,scales="free") +
  scale_color_manual(values = all_data_source_color_palette,
                     breaks = c("AIS plus S1",
                                "EIA",
                                "EDGAR"))

```


```{r eval = FALSE}
#| label: fig-co2-emissions-map
#| fig-cap: Spatial patterns of total fleet CO2 emissions in 2024 (top panel), and the change in CO2 emissions between 2016 and 2024 (bottom panel) (metric tonnes). Total fleet are shown at a 1x1 degree spatial resolution.
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 6
co2_emissions_map_fig <- annual_spatial_co2_emissions_ais_dark  |>
  # Using centroid of pixel, calculate area
  dplyr::mutate(pixel_area_km2 = lat_to_area(lat_bin+0.5)) |>
  dplyr::filter(year %in% c(2016,2024)) |>
  dplyr::mutate(across(c(emissions_co2_mt,emissions_co2_dark_mt), ~tidyr::replace_na(.,0))) |>
  # Calculate total emissions
  dplyr::mutate(emissions_co2_total_mt = emissions_co2_mt + emissions_co2_dark_mt) |>
  dplyr::select(-c(emissions_co2_mt,emissions_co2_dark_mt)) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = year,
                     values_from = emissions_co2_total_mt) |>
  dplyr::mutate(`Change from 2016 to 2024` = `2024`-`2016`) |>
  dplyr::select(-`2016`) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = c(`2024`,`Change from 2016 to 2024`)) |>
  ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = world_bbox_sf, fill = "black") +
  ggspatial::geom_spatial_tile(aes(x = lon_bin+0.5,
                                   y = lat_bin+0.5,
                                   fill = value,
                                   color = value)) +
  geom_tile(aes(x = lon_bin+0.5,
                y = lat_bin+0.5,
                fill = value,
                color = value)) +
  geom_sf(data = land,
          color = "#374a6d",
          fill = "#374a6d") +
  facet_wrap(name~., ncol=1) +
  scico::scale_fill_scico("Total fleet CO2 emissions (MT)",
                          palette = "berlin",
                          midpoint = 0,
                          trans = ggallin::pseudolog10_trans,
                          na.value = "black",
                          breaks = c(-1e5,
                                     -1e3,
                                     0,
                                     1e3,
                                     1e5,
                                     1e7),
                          labels=c("-100K",
                                   "-1k",
                                   "0",
                                   "1k",
                                   "100K",
                                   "10M")) +
  scico::scale_color_scico("Total fleet CO2 emissions (MT)",
                           palette = "berlin",
                           midpoint = 0,
                           trans = ggallin::pseudolog10_trans,
                           na.value = "black",
                           breaks = c(-1e5,
                                      -1e3,
                                      0,
                                      1e3,
                                      1e5,
                                      1e7),
                           labels=c("-100K",
                                    "-1k",
                                    "0",
                                    "1k",
                                    "100K",
                                    "10M")) +
  labs(x = '',
       y= '') +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.direction = "horizontal") +
  guides(fill = guide_colorbar(title.position = "top",
                               title.hjust = 0.5,
                               frame.colour = "black",
                               ticks.colour = "black",
                               barwidth = unit(5, "cm"))) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0, 1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 1)) +
  coord_sf(crs = map_projection)

co2_emissions_map_fig
```


```{r}
# For each pollutant, estimate how much emissions are underestimated by just using AIS
# As compared to using AIS plus S1
ais_scale_underestimation_2024 <- total_percent_change_by_fleet |>
  dplyr::filter(fleet != "S1 (non-broadcasting)") |>
  dplyr::select(fleet,pollutant,`2024`) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = "fleet",
                     values_from = `2024`) |>
  dplyr::mutate(ais_underestimation_2024 = (1 - `AIS (broadcasting)`/`AIS plus S1`)|>
                  scales::percent()) |>
  dplyr::rename(emissions_mt_2024_ais = `AIS (broadcasting)`,
                emissions_mt_2024_ais_s1 = `AIS plus S1`)

# For each pollutant, estimate how much the 2016->2024 trend in emissions is underestimated by just using AIS
# As compared to using AIS plus S1
ais_trend_overestimation_2016_2024 <- total_percent_change_by_fleet |>
  dplyr::filter(fleet != "S1 (non-broadcasting)") |>
  dplyr::select(fleet,pollutant,percent_change) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = "fleet",
                     values_from = percent_change) |>
  dplyr::mutate(trend_overestimation_2016_2024 = (`AIS (broadcasting)`/`AIS plus S1` -1)|>
                  scales::percent())|>
  dplyr::rename(trend_2016_2024_ais = `AIS (broadcasting)`,
                trend_2016_2024_ais_s1 = `AIS plus S1`)

# Put all this information together
pollutant_ais_underestimation_overestimation_summary <- ais_scale_underestimation_2024 |>
  dplyr::left_join(ais_trend_overestimation_2016_2024, by = "pollutant") |>
  dplyr::select(pollutant,ais_underestimation_2024,trend_overestimation_2016_2024)
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-total-annual-emissions-by-fleet
#| tbl-cap: Total global annual CO2 emissions (billion metric tonnes) by fleet for 2016 and 2024. The percent of total 2024 emissions for each fleet is also shown, along with the percent change from 2016 to 2024 for each fleet.

total_percent_change_by_fleet |>
  dplyr::filter(pollutant == "co2") |>
  dplyr::select(-pollutant) |>
  dplyr::mutate(percent_change = round(percent_change),
                percent_change = ifelse(percent_change>0,
                                        glue::glue("+{percent_change}%"),
                                        glue::glue("{percent_change}%"))) |>
  dplyr::relocate(fleet,`2016`,`2024`,`percent_total_2024`) |>
  dplyr::mutate(across(c(`2016`,`2024`), ~signif(./1e9,3))) |>
  knitr::kable()
```

## Figure 2

```{r}
#| label: fig-pollutant-emissions-map-time-series-vessel-type
#| fig-cap: (A) Spatial map of 2024 emissions by pollutant, aggregated across the AIS-broadcasting fleet and non-broadcasting, and shown at a 1x1 degree spatial resolution; (B) time series of monthly global emissions by pollutant, aggregated across the AIS-broadcasting fleet and non-broadcasting; (C) total 2024 CO2 emissions by vessel type for AIS-broadcasting (millions metric tonnes).
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 9

# For figures, order pollutants first for GHG pollutants,
# then local pollutants
pollutant_factor_levels <- c("CO2","CH4","N2O","CO","NOX","SOX","PM2.5","PM10","VOCs")
pollutant_map <-total_spatial_emissions_by_pollutant_2024 |>
  dplyr::mutate(pollutant = forcats::fct_relevel(pollutant,
                                                 pollutant_factor_levels)) |>
  dplyr::filter(pollutant %in% c("CO2","PM2.5")) |>
  ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = world_bbox_sf, fill = "black") +
  ggspatial::geom_spatial_tile(aes(x = lon_bin+0.5,
                                   y = lat_bin+0.5,
                                   color = emissions_mt,
                                   fill = emissions_mt))+
  geom_sf(data = land,
          color = "#374a6d",
          fill = "#374a6d") +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "mako",
                       name = "2024\n emissions\n(MT)",
                       breaks = c(0.0001,
                                  1,
                                  10000,
                                  10e6),
                       labels=c("0.0001",
                                "1",
                                "10k",
                                "10M"),
                       trans="log10")+
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "mako",
                        name = "2024\n emissions\n(MT)",
                        breaks = c(0.0001,
                                   1,
                                   10000,
                                   10e6),
                        labels=c("0.0001",
                                 "1",
                                 "10k",
                                 "10M"),
                        trans="log10") +
  labs(x = '',
       y= '') +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "left",
        legend.direction = "vertical",
        legend.key.height = unit(1.5, 'cm'),
        legend.key.width = unit(1, 'cm')) +
  guides(fill = guide_colorbar(title.position = "top",
                               title.hjust = 0.5,
                               frame.colour = "black",
                               ticks.colour = "black")) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0, 1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 1)) +
  facet_wrap(pollutant~.,ncol=1)

emissions_by_vessel_class <- annual_ais_co2_emissions_by_vessel |>
  dplyr::filter(year == 2024) |>
  dplyr::group_by(vessel_class) |>
  dplyr::summarise(emissions_co2_mt = sum(emissions_co2_mt)) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::mutate(fishing = ifelse(vessel_class %in% c("trawlers",
                                                     "squid_jigger",
                                                     "tuna_purse_seines",
                                                     "set_longlines",
                                                     "pole_and_line",
                                                     "set_gillnets",
                                                     "pots_and_traps",
                                                     "dredge_fishing",
                                                     "other_seines",
                                                     "other_purse_seines",
                                                     "trollers"),
                                 "Fishing","Non-fishing") |>
                  forcats::fct_rev()) |>
  dplyr::mutate(vessel_class = stringr::str_replace_all(vessel_class,"cargo.","cargo: ") |>
                  stringr::str_replace_all("_"," ") |>
                  stringr::str_to_sentence()) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = emissions_co2_mt,
             y = reorder(vessel_class,emissions_co2_mt))) +
  geom_col (position = position_dodge2(preserve = "single")) +
  ggforce::facet_col(~ fishing, space = "free", scales = "free") +
  theme_plot() +
  labs(title = "2024 CO2 emissions by vessel type (MT)",
       x = "",
       y = "") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::unit_format(unit = "M", scale = 1e-6), limits = c(0,NA)) 


pollutant_timeseries <- total_monthly_emissions_by_pollutant |> 
  dplyr::mutate(pollutant = forcats::fct_relevel(pollutant,
                                                 pollutant_factor_levels)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = month,
             y = emissions_mt)) +
  geom_line() + 
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  theme_plot() +
  labs(x = "",
       y = "Monthly emissions (MT)") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank())+
  facet_wrap(pollutant~.,ncol=3,scales="free_y")

cowplot::plot_grid(cowplot::plot_grid(pollutant_map, emissions_by_vessel_class,ncol=2,
                                      rel_widths = c(0.5,0.5),
                                      labels = c("","C")),
                   pollutant_timeseries, 
                   nrow = 2,rel_heights = c(0.55,0.45),
                   labels = c("A","B"))
```

## Figure 3

```{r}
#| label: build-decomposition-model

# For aggregate annual extrapolation dataset, calculated total emissions, kw-hours,distance, hours
annual_extrapolation_dataset <- annual_ais_to_dark_activity_extrapolation |>
  dplyr::mutate(emissions_co2_mt_total = emissions_co2_mt + emissions_co2_mt_dark,
                kw_hours_total = kw_hours + kw_hours_dark,
                distance_nm_total = distance_nm + distance_nm_dark,
                hours_total = hours + hours_dark) |>
  dplyr::select(-c(emissions_co2_mt, emissions_co2_mt_dark, 
                   kw_hours, kw_hours_dark, 
                   distance_nm, distance_nm_dark, 
                   hours, hours_dark, avg_speed_knots)) |>
  # Calculate average aggregate speed as total distance traveled divided by total hours
  dplyr::mutate(avg_speed_knots = distance_nm_total / hours_total)

# Using annual_extrapolation_dataset, use tidymodels to train a random forest model
# to predict emissions
set.seed(123)

# Set initial split of data
inital_split <- annual_extrapolation_dataset |>
  rsample::initial_split(prop = 0.8)

training_data <-rsample::training(inital_split)
testing_data <- rsample::testing(inital_split)

# Specify recipe for model
model_recipe <- recipes::recipe(emissions_co2_mt_total ~ fishing + length_size_class_percentile + kw_hours_total + avg_speed_knots, 
                                data = head(rsample::training(inital_split))) |>
  recipes::step_num2factor(length_size_class_percentile,
                           levels = as.character(unique(annual_extrapolation_dataset$length_size_class_percentile)))

# Specify model type
model_type <- parsnip::rand_forest(trees = 1000,
                                   # Default of ranger is squareroot of number of model features. We have 4 features, so use a value of 2.
                                   mtry = 2,
                                   # Default of ranger is 5 for regression
                                   min_n = 5) |>
  parsnip::set_engine("ranger") |>
  parsnip::set_mode("regression")

# Initialize model workflow
model_workflow <- workflows::workflow() |>
  workflows::add_recipe(model_recipe) |>
  workflows::add_model(model_type)

# Fit model on training data
workflow_fit <- parsnip::fit(model_workflow,
                             training_data)

# Make predictions on testing data, and augment this to testing data frame
worflow_predictions <- parsnip::augment(workflow_fit,
                                        testing_data)


# Calculate performance metrics

# Multiple regression metrics
multi_metric <- yardstick::metric_set(yardstick::rsq, 
                                      yardstick::mape,
                                      yardstick::rsq_trad)

performance_metrics <- multi_metric(data = worflow_predictions,
                                    truth = emissions_co2_mt_total,
                                    estimate = .pred) |>
  dplyr::select(-.estimator)

# Final model on all data
# Fit final model on all data
workflow_fit_final <- parsnip::fit(model_workflow,
                                   annual_extrapolation_dataset)
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-decomposition-model-performance
#| tbl-cap: Performance metrics for random forest model that predicts annual CO2 emissions, by vessel size class and type, using hours and average speed.

performance_metrics |>
  knitr::kable(digits = 3)
```

```{r}
#| label: predict-using-decomposition-model
# Find initial/starting 2016 values of kw-hours and speed, by fishing and length_size_class_percentile, for decomposition
starting_values <- annual_extrapolation_dataset |>
  dplyr::filter(year == 2016) |>
  dplyr::select(fishing,
                length_size_class_percentile,
                kw_hours_total_starting = kw_hours_total,
                hours_total_starting = hours_total,
                avg_speed_knots_starting = avg_speed_knots)

# Add starting values to predictions dataset
predictions_dataset <- annual_extrapolation_dataset |>
  left_join(starting_values, by  = c("fishing","length_size_class_percentile")) 

# Now run predictions for 4 different scenarios
final_prediction_dataset <- predictions_dataset |>
  # Add model predictions using observed kw-hours and observed speed
  bind_cols(observed_kw_hours_observed_speed = predict(workflow_fit_final, predictions_dataset)$.pred) |>
  # Add model predictions using observed kw-hours and constant speed (from 2016)
  bind_cols(observed_kw_hours_constant_speed = predict(workflow_fit_final, predictions_dataset  |>
                                                         dplyr::select(-avg_speed_knots) |>
                                                         dplyr::rename(avg_speed_knots = avg_speed_knots_starting))$.pred)|>
  # Add model predictions using constant kw-hours (from 2016) and observed speed
  bind_cols(constant_kw_hours_observed_speed = predict(workflow_fit_final, predictions_dataset|>
                                                         dplyr::select(-kw_hours_total) |>
                                                         dplyr::rename(kw_hours_total = kw_hours_total_starting))$.pred)|>
  # Add model predictions using constant kw-hours (from 2016) and constant speed (from 2016)
  bind_cols(constant_kw_hours_constant_speed = predict(workflow_fit_final, predictions_dataset |>
                                                         dplyr::select(-avg_speed_knots) |>
                                                         dplyr::rename(avg_speed_knots = avg_speed_knots_starting) |>
                                                         dplyr::select(-kw_hours_total) |>
                                                         dplyr::rename(kw_hours_total = kw_hours_total_starting))$.pred)
```

## Figure 3 panel A

```{r}
# Aggregate prediction values by year (across vessel size classes and type)
final_annual_prediction_dataset <- final_prediction_dataset |>
  dplyr::group_by(year) |>
  dplyr::summarise(across(c(observed_kw_hours_observed_speed,
                            observed_kw_hours_constant_speed,
                            constant_kw_hours_observed_speed,
                            constant_kw_hours_constant_speed),
                          ~sum(.,na.rm=TRUE))) |>
  dplyr::rename(`Observed kW-hours, observed speed` = observed_kw_hours_observed_speed,
                `Observed kW-hours, constant speed` = observed_kw_hours_constant_speed,
                `Constant kW-hours, observed speed` = constant_kw_hours_observed_speed,
                `Constant kW-hours, constant speed` = constant_kw_hours_constant_speed) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-year) 

# We will normalize all of these predictions to the 2016 starting prediction
starting_value_from_prediction <- final_annual_prediction_dataset |>
  dplyr::filter(year == 2016) |>
  rename(starting_value = value) |>
  dplyr::select(-year)

time_series_predicted_decomposition_df <- final_annual_prediction_dataset |>
  dplyr::inner_join(starting_value_from_prediction, by = "name") |>
  dplyr::mutate(normalized_value = value/starting_value-1)

time_series_predicted_decomposition <- time_series_predicted_decomposition_df |>
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = normalized_value, color = name)) +
  geom_line() +
  guides(color = guide_legend(reverse=TRUE)) +
  scale_color_manual("Prediction scenario",
                     values = c("coral3",
                                "coral4",
                                paletteer::paletteer_d("colorblindr::OkabeIto")[[2]],
                                paletteer::paletteer_d("colorblindr::OkabeIto")[[5]])) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(final_prediction_dataset$year)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  labs(x = "",
       y = "",
       title = "Predicted relative change in total CO2 emissions from 2016 baseline") +
  theme_plot() +
  theme(panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank())
```

## Figure 3 Panel B

```{r}
annual_vessel_summary_by_year <- annual_ais_co2_emissions_by_vessel |>
  dplyr::group_by(year) |>
  dplyr::summarize(n_distinct_vessels = dplyr::n_distinct(ssvid),
                   kw_hours = sum(hours * main_engine_power_kw),
                   hours = sum(hours),
                   main_engine_power_kw = sum(main_engine_power_kw)) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::mutate(kw_per_vessel = main_engine_power_kw / n_distinct_vessels,
                hours_per_vessel = hours / n_distinct_vessels) |>
  dplyr::select(-c(hours,main_engine_power_kw)) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-year) |>
  # Limit to just years 2016 and beyond, to match decomposition
  # Since we don't have dark data for 2015
  dplyr::filter(year>2015) |>
  dplyr::mutate(name = dplyr::case_when(name == "hours" ~ "Total hours",
                                        name == "kw_hours" ~ "Total kW-hours",
                                        name == "main_engine_power_kw" ~ "Total main engine power (KW)",
                                        name == "kw_per_vessel" ~ "Average main engine power per vessel (kW)",
                                        name == "hours_per_vessel" ~ "Average hours per vessel",
                                        name == "n_distinct_vessels" ~ "Unique number of vessels") |>
                  forcats::fct_relevel("Total kW-hours","Unique number of vessels"))

annual_vessel_summary_starting <- annual_vessel_summary_by_year |>
  dplyr::filter(year == 2016) |>
  dplyr::select(-year) |>
  dplyr::rename(value_starting = value)

ais_fleet_summary_absolute <- annual_vessel_summary_by_year |>
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = value)) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(name~., ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels =  scales::unit_format(unit = "k", scale = 1e-3),
                     limits = c(0,NA)) + 
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(annual_vessel_summary_by_year$year)) +
  theme_plot() +
  labs(x = "",
       y = "",
       title = "Observed metrics from\nAIS data") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

ais_fleet_summary_relative_df <- annual_vessel_summary_by_year |>
  dplyr::left_join(annual_vessel_summary_starting, by = "name") |>
  dplyr::mutate(value_normalized = value/value_starting-1) 

ais_fleet_summary_relative <- ais_fleet_summary_relative_df |>
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = value_normalized)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = 2) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(name~., ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent,limits=c(-.2,0.8)) + 
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(annual_vessel_summary_by_year$year)) +
  theme_plot() +
  labs(x = "",
       y = "",
       title = "Observed relative change in\nAIS metrics from 2016 baseline") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

ais_fleet_summary_relative_df_2024 <- ais_fleet_summary_relative_df |>
  dplyr::filter(year==2024)
```


## Figure 3 combined

```{r}
#| label: fig-hours-speed-decomposition
#| fig-cap: (A) Relative predicted change in total CO2 emissions from 2016 baseline (aggregating across both broadcasting and non-broadcasting vessels), using the random forest model outputs of four different scenarios that decompose the relative impact of changes in total kW-hours and changes in average speed ; (B) Observed total kW-hours for AIS-broadcasting vessels, number of unique vessels, average hours per vessel, and average main engine power per vessel; (C) Relative change from a 2016 baseline for observed total kW-hours for AIS-broadcasting vessels, number of unique vessels, average hours per vessel, and average main engine power per vessel.
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 7.5

cowplot::plot_grid(time_series_predicted_decomposition,
                   cowplot::plot_grid(ais_fleet_summary_absolute,
                                      ais_fleet_summary_relative,
                                      ncol = 2,
                                      align = "h",
                                      labels = c("B","C"),
                                      rel_widths = c(0.53,0.47)),
                   ncol=1,rel_heights = c(0.4,0.6),
                   labels = c("A",""))
```


Looking at the observed AIS data, the number of unique vessels increased by `r ais_fleet_summary_relative_df_2024 |> dplyr::filter(name == "Unique number of vessels") |> dplyr::pull(value_normalized) |> scales::percent()` from 2016 to 2024, while the average number of hours per vessel increased by only `r ais_fleet_summary_relative_df_2024 |> dplyr::filter(name == "Average hours per vessel") |> dplyr::pull(value_normalized) |> scales::percent()` and the average main engine power per vessel actually decreased by `r ais_fleet_summary_relative_df_2024 |> dplyr::filter(name == "Average main engine power per vessel (kW)") |> dplyr::pull(value_normalized) |> abs() |> scales::percent()`. This suggests that most of the observed `r ais_fleet_summary_relative_df_2024 |> dplyr::filter(name == "Total kW-hours") |> dplyr::pull(value_normalized) |> scales::percent()` increase in kW-hours from 2016 to 2024 was driven by the increase in vessels, not by changes in how much each vessel operates or the engine size of each vessel. 

# Abstract text with stats

We develop a method for quantifying high-resolution spatiotemporal  marine vessel emissions at a global scale by fusing two unique satellite-based data sources: 1) Automatic Identification System (AIS) to estimate vessel-level emissions for the over `r prettyNum(signif(length(unique(annual_ais_co2_emissions_by_vessel$ssvid)),3),big.mark=",")` boats that broadcast their positions using AIS; and 2) synthetic aperture radar (SAR) data from the Sentinel-1 satellite constellation to estimate emissions for boats that do not use AIS. We find that vessels emitted approximately `r (edgar_comparison|>dplyr::filter(name=="emissions_co2_billion_mt_ocean_gfw"))$value` billion metric tons of CO2 in 2023, accounting for `r (edgar_comparison|>dplyr::filter(name=="fraction_ocean_over_total"))$value` of global fossil fuel emissions from the transportation sector. We also find that marine CO2 emissions increased by `r (edgar_comparison|>dplyr::filter(name=="emissions_normalized_from_2016_ocean_gfw"))$value` between 2016 and 2023, `r (edgar_comparison|>dplyr::filter(name=="normalized_ocean_over_land"))$value` times the increase measured from terrestrial transportation emissions. Data fusion is critical to getting these numbers right: if only AIS data were used, the currently accepted state-of- the-art for measuring vessel emissions, global emissions in 2024 would be *underestimated* by `r pollutant_ais_underestimation_overestimation_summary |> dplyr::filter(pollutant == "co2") |> dplyr::pull(ais_underestimation_2024)`, and the increasing trend from 2016 to 2024 would be *overestimated* by `r pollutant_ais_underestimation_overestimation_summary |> dplyr::filter(pollutant == "co2") |> dplyr::pull(trend_overestimation_2016_2024)`.

# Introduction text with stats

```{r}
percent_2023_emissions_from_transportation <- (annual_edgar_tranportation_emissions |>
                                                 dplyr::mutate(transportation = ifelse(sector %in% c("Civil Aviation",
                                                                                                     "Road Transportation no resuspension",
                                                                                                     "Other Transportation",
                                                                                                     "Railways","Water-borne Navigation"), TRUE,FALSE)) |> 
                                                 dplyr::filter(year == 2023) |> 
                                                 dplyr::group_by(transportation) |> 
                                                 dplyr::summarize(emissions_co2_mt = sum(emissions_co2_mt,na.rm=TRUE)) |> 
                                                 dplyr::ungroup() |> 
                                                 tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = "transportation",values_from = "emissions_co2_mt") |>
                                                 dplyr::mutate(fraction_transportation_of_total = `TRUE`/(`TRUE` + `FALSE`)))$fraction_transportation_of_total |>
  scales::percent()
```

In 2023, the transportation sector was responsible for `r percent_2023_emissions_from_transportation` of all emissions globally

## Social Cost of Carbon externality text

```{r}
unctad_anual_global_trade_value_2021_usd <- unctad_annual_global_trade_value_data_maritime_transport |>
  dplyr::filter(year == 2021) |> 
  dplyr::pull(value_usd) 
# CO2 emissions by non-fishing vessels in 2021
co2_emissions_2021_non_fishing_mt <- annual_emissions_all_pollutants_with_total |> 
  dplyr::filter(pollutant == "co2", 
                !fishing, 
                year == 2021, 
                fleet == "Total") |> 
  dplyr::pull(emissions_mt)

# Social cost of carbon per metric tonne of CO2
# From https://www.pnas.org/doi/10.1073/pnas.2410733121
scc_usd_mt_co2 <- 283

scc_co2_emissions_2021_usd <- co2_emissions_2021_non_fishing_mt * scc_usd_mt_co2
```


In 2021, the total global value of commercial maritime transportation was $`r unctad_anual_global_trade_value_2021_usd |> signif(3) / 1e12 ` trillion USD. Looking at our emissions estimates, we find that non-fishing vessels emitted `r  co2_emissions_2021_non_fishing_mt |> signif(3)/1e9` billion metric tonnes of CO2. Using a SCC value of \$283 USD/metric tonne of CO2, this translates to a total SCC in 2021 of \$USD `r scc_co2_emissions_2021_usd |> signif(3) / 1e9` billion USD for emissions from the marine transportation sector, representing `r scales::percent(scc_co2_emissions_2021_usd/unctad_anual_global_trade_value_2021_usd)` of the total value of goods transported by maritime trade.

# Supplementary tables

```{r}
total_percent_change_by_fleet_tbl <- total_percent_change_by_fleet |>
  dplyr::mutate(across(c(`2016`,`2024`,percent_change), ~signif(.,3))) |>
  dplyr::mutate(pollutant = stringr::str_to_upper(pollutant) |>
                  stringr::str_replace_all("_","."),
                pollutant = ifelse(pollutant == "VOCS","VOCs",pollutant)) |>
  dplyr::rename(`Data source` = fleet,
                Pollutant = pollutant,
                `2016 emissions (MT)` = `2016`,
                `2024 emissions (MT)` = `2024`,
                `Percent of 2024 total` = percent_total_2024,
                `Percent change from 2016 to 2024` = percent_change) |>
  knitr::kable(format = 'latex', label = "total_percent_change_by_fleet_tbl")

writeLines(total_percent_change_by_fleet_tbl, here::here("qmd/tables/total_percent_change_by_fleet_tbl.tex"))
```


# Supplementary figures

```{r}
#| label: fig-total-annual-unique-vessels-hours-power
#| fig-cap: Annual total CO2 emissions (metric tonnes); total operating hours; total engine power across vessels (killowatt); number distinct operating vessels; and average speed; across all AIS-broadcasting. The percent change from 2015 to 2024 is shown next to each line.
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 6
# Standard error function
# https://www.r-bloggers.com/2022/03/calculate-standard-error-in-r/
std <- function(x) sd(x)/sqrt(length(x))

annual_vessel_summary_by_year <- annual_ais_co2_emissions_by_vessel |>
  dplyr::group_by(year) |>
  dplyr::summarize(n_distinct_vessels = dplyr::n_distinct(ssvid),
                   dplyr::across(c(emissions_co2_mt,
                                   main_engine_power_kw,
                                   hours), c(mean = mean,sum = sum,std = std)),
                   dplyr::across(speed_knots, c(mean = mean, std = std))) |>
  dplyr::ungroup()   |>
  dplyr::mutate(hours_per_vessel = hours_sum / n_distinct_vessels) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-year)

total_percent_change_vessel_summary  <-annual_vessel_summary_by_year |>
  dplyr::filter(year %in% max(year) | year %in% min(year)) |>
  dplyr::arrange(year) |> 
  dplyr::group_by(name) |> 
  dplyr::summarize(end_value = value[2],
                   percent_change = round(100*(value[2] - value[1])/value[1])) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::mutate(percent_change = ifelse(percent_change>0,
                                        glue::glue("+{percent_change}%"),
                                        glue::glue("{percent_change}%")))

annual_vessel_summary_by_year|>
  dplyr::filter(name %in% c("hours_sum","emissions_co2_mt_sum","n_distinct_vessels","main_engine_power_kw_sum","speed_knots_mean","hours_per_vessel")) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = value)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_text(data = total_percent_change_vessel_summary|>
              dplyr::filter(name %in% c("hours_sum","emissions_co2_mt_sum","n_distinct_vessels","main_engine_power_kw_sum","speed_knots_mean","hours_per_vessel")) ,
            aes(label = percent_change,
                y = end_value,
                x = max(annual_vessel_summary_by_year$year)+.5),
            show.legend = FALSE)+
  facet_wrap(name~., ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0,NA)) + 
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(annual_vessel_summary_by_year$year)) +
  theme_plot() +
  labs(x = "",
       y = "") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank())
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-total-indicators-by-earliest-operation-year
#| fig-cap: Total global CO2 emissions; number unique vessels; operating hours; and total main engine power for the set of AIS-broadcasting that have been operating since a given year, where each line is colored by the earliest operation year. The percent change from the earliest year to 2024 is shown next to its line.
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 9



# For vessels that have been in operation since a given year, calculate their annual emissions
# Do this for every where from 2015 to 2022
annual_emissions_by_fleet_year <- purrr::map_df(seq(min(annual_ais_co2_emissions_by_vessel$year),
                                                    max(annual_ais_co2_emissions_by_vessel$year)-1),
                                                function(year_tmp){
                                                  # Find those vessels that have been operating since this
                                                  year_fleet <- annual_ais_co2_emissions_by_vessel |>
                                                    dplyr::filter(year == year_tmp) |>
                                                    dplyr::select(ssvid)
                                                  # For these vessels, calculate annual emissions
                                                  annual_ais_co2_emissions_by_vessel |>
                                                    dplyr::filter(year >= year_tmp) |>
                                                    dplyr::inner_join(year_fleet, by = "ssvid") |>
                                                    dplyr::group_by(year) |>
                                                    dplyr::summarize(dplyr::across(c(emissions_co2_mt,
                                                                                     main_engine_power_kw,
                                                                                     hours), c(mean = mean,sum = sum,std = std, median = median)),
                                                                     dplyr::across(speed_knots, c(mean = mean, median = median)),
                                                                     n_distinct_vessels = dplyr::n_distinct(ssvid)) |>
                                                    dplyr::ungroup() |>
                                                    dplyr::mutate(fleet_year = year_tmp)
                                                } )   |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-c(fleet_year,year)) 

# Calculate the total percent change in emissions by each operation year fleet
total_percent_change_by_fleet_year  <-annual_emissions_by_fleet_year |>
  dplyr::group_by(fleet_year) |>
  dplyr::filter(year %in% max(year) | year %in% min(year)) |>
  dplyr::arrange(year) |> 
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::group_by(name,fleet_year) |> 
  dplyr::summarize(end_value = value[2],
                   percent_change = round(100*(value[2] - value[1])/value[1])) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::mutate(percent_change = ifelse(percent_change>0,
                                        glue::glue("+{percent_change}%"),
                                        glue::glue("{percent_change}%")))

annual_emissions_by_fleet_year   |>
  dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(name,"sum") | stringr::str_detect(name,"vessels")) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = value, color = as.factor(fleet_year), group = fleet_year)) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(name~., ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  geom_text(data = total_percent_change_by_fleet_year|>
              dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(name,"sum") | stringr::str_detect(name,"vessels")),
            aes(label = percent_change,
                y = end_value,
                x = max(annual_emissions_by_fleet_year$year)+.5),
            show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma,
                     limits = c(0,NA)) +
  paletteer::scale_color_paletteer_d(palette = "jcolors::pal10",
                                     guide = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(annual_emissions_by_fleet_year$year)) +
  theme_plot() +
  labs(x = "",
       y = "",
       color = "Earliest\noperation\nyear") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank())
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-individual-distribution-annual-emissions-hours-power
#| fig-cap: Annual distributions of individual per-vessel CO2 emissions (metric tonnes); operating hours; main engine power (killowatts); and average speed (knots). The point shows the mean value, and the error bars show +/- two standard errors.
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 5

annual_vessel_summary_by_year |>
  dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(name,"mean|std")) |>
  dplyr::mutate(indicator_type = ifelse(stringr::str_detect(name,"mean"),"mean","std")) |>
  dplyr::mutate(name = stringr::str_remove_all(name,"_mean"))|>
  dplyr::mutate(name = stringr::str_remove_all(name,"_std")) |>
  dplyr::filter(name %in% c("emissions_co2_mt","hours","main_engine_power_kw","speed_knots")) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = indicator_type,
                     values_from = value) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = mean, group = year))+ 
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean - 2*std, ymax = mean + 2*std)) +
  facet_wrap(name~.,scales="free_y",ncol=1) +
  theme_plot()  +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(annual_ais_co2_emissions_by_vessel$year)) +
  theme_plot() +
  labs(x = "",
       y = "") 
```


```{r}
#| label: fig-individual-distribution-annual-emissions-hours-power-by-earliest-operation-year
#| fig-cap: Annual mean of individual per-vessel CO2 emissions (metric tonnes); operating hours; main engine power (killowatts); and average speed (knots) for the set of AIS-broadcasting that have been operating since a given year, where each line is colored by the earliest operation year. The percent change from the earliest year to 2024 is shown next to its line.
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 8

annual_emissions_by_fleet_year  |>
  dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(name,"mean")) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = value, group = fleet_year, color = as.factor(fleet_year)))+ 
  geom_line()  +
  geom_text(data = total_percent_change_by_fleet_year|>
              dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(name,"mean")),
            aes(label = percent_change,
                y = end_value,
                x = max(annual_emissions_by_fleet_year$year)+.5),
            show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(name~.,scales="free_y",ncol=1) +
  theme_plot()  +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(annual_emissions_by_fleet_year$year)) +
  theme_plot() +
  labs(x = "",
       y = "",
       color = "Earliest\noperation\nyear") +
  paletteer::scale_color_paletteer_d(palette = "jcolors::pal10") 
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-first-last-operation-year-histogram
#| fig-cap: Histograms showing the distribution of the first and last operation year of each vessel.
#| 
# Determine entry and exit year for each vessel
first_last_year_by_vessel <- annual_ais_co2_emissions_by_vessel |>
  dplyr::group_by(ssvid) |>
  dplyr::summarize(first_operation_year = min(year),
                   last_operation_year = max(year)) |>
  dplyr::ungroup()

first_last_year_by_vessel |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-ssvid) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = value)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = length(unique(annual_ais_co2_emissions_by_vessel$year)), color = "black") +
  facet_wrap(name~., ncol = 1) +
  facet_wrap(name~.,scales="free_y",ncol=1) +
  theme_plot()  +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(annual_ais_co2_emissions_by_vessel$year)) +
  theme_plot() +
  labs(x = "",
       y = "Number vessels")+
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
```


```{r}
#| label: fig-individual-distribution-annual-emissions-hours-power-by-first-year
#| fig-cap: Annual mean of individual per-vessel CO2 emissions (metric tonnes); mean per-vessel operating hours; mean per-vessel main engine power (killowatts); mean per-vessel speed (knots); and total number of distinct vessels for the set of AIS-broadcasting for each first year of operation, where each line is colored by the first operation year.
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 8

indicators_by_first_operation_year <- purrr::map_df(seq(min(first_last_year_by_vessel$first_operation_year),
                                                        max(first_last_year_by_vessel$first_operation_year)),
                                                    function(year_tmp){
                                                      
                                                      year_fleet <- first_last_year_by_vessel |>
                                                        dplyr::filter(first_operation_year == year_tmp) 
                                                      # For these vessels, calculate annual emissions
                                                      annual_ais_co2_emissions_by_vessel |>
                                                        dplyr::inner_join(year_fleet, by = "ssvid") |>
                                                        dplyr::group_by(year) |>
                                                        dplyr::summarize(dplyr::across(c(emissions_co2_mt,
                                                                                         main_engine_power_kw,
                                                                                         hours), c(mean = mean,sum = sum,std = std, median = median)),
                                                                         dplyr::across(speed_knots, c(mean = mean, median = median)),
                                                                         n_distinct_vessels = dplyr::n_distinct(ssvid)) |>
                                                        dplyr::ungroup() |>
                                                        dplyr::mutate(fleet_year = year_tmp)
                                                    }) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-c(year,fleet_year))

indicators_by_first_operation_year  |>
  dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(name,"mean") |
                  stringr::str_detect(name,"vessel"))  |>
  dplyr::mutate(name = forcats::fct_relevel(name,"main_engine_power_kw_mean", after = Inf)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = value, group = fleet_year, color = as.factor(fleet_year)))+ 
  geom_line()  +
  facet_wrap(name~.,scales="free_y",ncol=1) +
  theme_plot()  +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(annual_emissions_by_fleet_year$year)) +
  theme_plot() +
  labs(x = "",
       y = "",
       color = "First\noperation\nyear") +
  paletteer::scale_color_paletteer_d(palette = "jcolors::pal10")  +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-individual-distribution-annual-emissions-hours-power-by-last-year
#| fig-cap: Annual mean of individual per-vessel CO2 emissions (metric tonnes); mean per-vessel operating hours; mean per-vessel main engine power (killowatts); mean per-vessel speed (knots); and total number of distinct vessels for the set of AIS-broadcasting for each last year of operation, where each line is colored by the last operation year.
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 8

indicators_by_last_operation_year <- purrr::map_df(seq(min(first_last_year_by_vessel$last_operation_year),
                                                       max(first_last_year_by_vessel$last_operation_year)),
                                                   function(year_tmp){
                                                     
                                                     year_fleet <- first_last_year_by_vessel |>
                                                       dplyr::filter(last_operation_year == year_tmp) 
                                                     # For these vessels, calculate annual emissions
                                                     annual_ais_co2_emissions_by_vessel |>
                                                       dplyr::inner_join(year_fleet, by = "ssvid") |>
                                                       dplyr::group_by(year) |>
                                                       dplyr::summarize(dplyr::across(c(emissions_co2_mt,
                                                                                        main_engine_power_kw,
                                                                                        hours), c(mean = mean,sum = sum,std = std, median = median)),
                                                                        dplyr::across(speed_knots, c(mean = mean, median = median)),
                                                                        n_distinct_vessels = dplyr::n_distinct(ssvid)) |>
                                                       dplyr::ungroup() |>
                                                       dplyr::mutate(fleet_year = year_tmp)
                                                   }) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-c(year,fleet_year))

indicators_by_last_operation_year  |>
  dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(name,"mean") |
                  stringr::str_detect(name,"vessel")) |>
  dplyr::mutate(name = forcats::fct_relevel(name,"main_engine_power_kw_mean", after = Inf)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = value, group = fleet_year, color = as.factor(fleet_year)))+ 
  geom_line()  +
  facet_wrap(name~.,scales="free_y",ncol=1) +
  theme_plot()  +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(annual_emissions_by_fleet_year$year)) +
  theme_plot() +
  labs(x = "",
       y = "",
       color = "Last\noperation\nyear") +
  paletteer::scale_color_paletteer_d(palette = "jcolors::pal10") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-annual-emissions-by-ais-receiver-type
#| fig-cap: Annual total CO2 emissions (metric tonnes) by the AIS-broadcasting fleet, disaggregated by AIS receiver type (satellite, terrestrial, and dynamic) and total aggregated across receiver types. A dashed vertical line is shown for 2022, the first year in which dynamic AIS was widely adopted.
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 6
annual_global_emissions_by_receiver_type|>
  dplyr::rename(`CO2 emissions (MT)` = emissions_co2_mt,
                `Number unique vessels` = n_distinct_ssvid) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = -c(year,receiver_type)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = year,
             y = value,
             color = receiver_type)) +
  facet_wrap(name~., scales = "free_y", ncol = 1) +
  geom_line() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::unit_format(unit = "M", scale = 1e-6)) + 
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(annual_global_emissions_by_receiver_type$year)) +
  scale_color_brewer(type = "qual",
                     palette = "Set2",
                     guide = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE) ) +
  theme_plot() +
  labs(x = "",
       y = "",
       color = "AIS receiver type") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank())+
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2022, linetype = 2) 
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-annual-emissions-by-ais-receiver-type-top-flags
#| fig-cap: Annual total CO2 emissions (metric tonnes) by the AIS-broadcasting fleet, disaggregated by AIS receiver type (satellite, terrestrial, and dynamic) and total aggregated across receiver types, for the top 10 flags with the most emissions in 2024. A dashed vertical line is shown for 2022, the first year in which dynamic AIS was widely adopted.
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 6

top_n_flags_2024 <- annual_global_emissions_by_receiver_type_and_flag |>
  dplyr::filter(year == 2024, receiver_type == "Total") |>
  dplyr::slice_max(n = 10, order_by = emissions_co2_mt) |>
  dplyr::select(flag)

annual_global_emissions_by_receiver_type_and_flag |>
  dplyr::inner_join(top_n_flags_2024, by = "flag") |>
  dplyr::mutate(flag = forcats::fct_relevel(flag,top_n_flags_2024$flag)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = year,
             y = emissions_co2_mt,
             color = flag)) +
  facet_grid(receiver_type~., scales = "free_y") +
  geom_line() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::unit_format(unit = "M", scale = 1e-6)) + 
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(annual_global_emissions_by_receiver_type$year)) +
  theme_plot() +
  labs(x = "",
       y = "CO2 emissions (MT)",
       color = "Flag") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank()) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2022, linetype = 2) +
  paletteer::scale_color_paletteer_d(palette = "rcartocolor::Safe")
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-annual-unique-vessels-by-ais-receiver-type-top-flags
```
